entities.name: MILLETS
entities.description: Grains that undergo post-harvest handling to minimize loss and increase value

entities.name: THRESHING
entities.description: The process of separating grains from the husks or straw after harvest

entities.name: DRYING
entities.description: The process of removing moisture from grains to prevent spoilage and contamination

entities.name: PROCESSING
entities.description: The conversion of raw millets into consumables through various techniques

entities.name: STORAGE
entities.description: The preservation of grains after harvest to maintain quality and longevity

entities.name: POST-HARVEST LOSS
entities.description: The reduction in quantity or quality of crops occurring between harvest and consumption

entities.name: DEHULLING
entities.description: A basic processing method to remove the outer shell of the grain

entities.name: MILLING
entities.description: A basic processing method to grind grains into flour or smaller particles

entities.name: EXTRUSION
entities.description: A sophisticated processing technology used to create value-added consumables

entities.name: FERMENTATION
entities.description: A biochemical processing technique used to enhance millet products

entities.name: ENZYMATIC TREATMENTS
entities.description: The use of enzymes to modify the properties of millets during processing

entities.name: HYGROSCOPIC QUALITY
entities.description: The ability of millets to absorb moisture from the environment

entities.name: MOLD GROWTH
entities.description: Fungal growth occurring in grains due to improper drying

entities.name: DISCOLORATION
entities.description: Change in the natural color of grains caused by poor post-harvest handling

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: Contamination of grains by toxic metabolites produced by certain molds

entities.name: LIPID OXIDATION
entities.description: The chemical breakdown of fats in millet flour that affects shelf life

entities.name: SHELF LIFE
entities.description: The length of time that millet products remain fit for consumption

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
entities.description: AI-driven systems used to improve productivity, accuracy, and quality assurance in post-harvest handling

entities.name: SORTING
entities.description: The process of separating grains based on specific quality criteria

entities.name: GRADING
entities.description: The process of categorizing grains into different quality levels

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTROL
entities.description: The management of water content in grains to ensure stability

entities.name: PHOTOVOLTAIC DRYERS
entities.description: Solar-powered drying systems used to preserve seed hydration levels

entities.name: HYDRAULIC DRYERS
entities.description: Dryers utilizing hydraulic systems to maintain ideal seed hydration

entities.name: SEED HYDRATION LEVELS
entities.description: The amount of water contained within the seed, critical for protection and longevity
